description_long: This is an irregular, flat stone slab with a predominantly brownish-gray coloration and several large, lighter patches of white and cream. The surface is uneven, with visible natural pitting, rough edges, and a slightly undulating top. The stone appears to be sedimentary or possibly a weathered piece of flint, with some areas showing a waxy, semi-translucent quality. The edges are jagged and angular, suggesting it was broken from a larger mass rather than cut or shaped. Its overall form is elongated and relatively thin, making it suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban landscape. The stone's surface shows signs of natural weathering, with some discoloration and minor surface erosion.
description: An irregular, flat stone slab with rough, jagged edges and a mix of brown, gray, and white tones. The surface is uneven and weathered, featuring natural pitting and lighter patches.
description_view_0: The stone appears elongated with a prominent white patch on the upper surface and a rough, angular edge visible on the right.
description_view_1: This view highlights the stone's thickness and a large cream-colored area along the side, with the top surface showing more texture.
description_view_2: The end of the stone is visible, showing a blocky, angular profile and a mix of brown and white coloration.
description_view_3: The top surface is most visible here, displaying a large, irregular white patch and a generally flat, pitted texture.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and veining with large, diffuse white and cream patches against a brown-gray base; no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and pitted, with visible signs of natural weathering and minor erosion, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground cover or gravel, but can contrast with paved surfaces or manicured lawns; may be partially embedded in soil or surrounded by grass.
